item:
Schema/database inclusion/exclusion
(can the user specify what schema/database to include/exclude from a table to be replicated?)

Table inclusion/exclusion
(can the user specify what tables to include/exclude in the replication process?)

Column inclusion/exclusion
(can the user specify what fields/columns to include/exclude from a table to be replicated?)

Availability of source database
(can the engine do replication without putting too much pressure on the source database; i.e. thousands of connections; thousands of binlog reads?)

item:
Minimal manual intervention
(can users use a single line of code to synchronize the database; for example in SQL syntax; [see Alibaba CDAS & CTAS advanced features](https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/en/realtime-compute-for-apache-flink/latest/create-database-as-statement))
Flink: 4* Table/SQL API: users need to define a DDL and query for each table

item: Non-invasiveness (the solution should not require major work on the source database to support CDC [Link](http://essay.utwente.nl/90447/1/Li_MA_EEMCS.pdf))
Debezium: 1) Requires setup transaction log 2) Requires locks 3) [May require write access (to create signalling table)](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/2.1/connectors/mysql.html#mysql-ad-hoc-snapshots)

item:
Lock-free reading
(can the engine avoid acquiring a global read lock during snapshotting and thus avoid user lock permissions)

Snapshot resilience
(can snapshots be aborted without inconsistencies, preferably restarting from a checkpoint?)
